DISCOVERY 302,DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED. MOTION GRANTED. THE COURT REQUESTS THAT DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MAKE GREATER EFFORTS TO LOCATE THE PLAINTIFFS TO DETERMINE WHETHER THIS CASE SHOULD PROCEED AT ALL, GIVEN THE APPARENT LACK OF PARTICIPATION SINCE REMOVAL OF COUNSEL. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: JULIA CAMPINS, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Aug-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Raymond Rollan, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3888, San Francisco City ATtorney's Office for Defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted.

Motion Granted. The Court requests that Defendant City and County of San Francisco make greater efforts to locate the Plaintiffs to determine whether this case should proceed at all, given the apparent lack of participation since removal of counsel.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: Julia Campins, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
